metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (What section and/or AC #3) that `setup-project.sh` must include a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'`-if-absent block (with `autolander-bot` as initial member and Administrators as subgroup/owner) *before* the `want=` UUID-resolution step, mirroring the existing `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` create-group patterns.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict (AC #3) but is silent on the preceding `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` convergence block that must run before UUID resolution. The existing script (lines 47–107) shows the invariant: every group in `project.config` must have a `create-group`-if-absent + `set-members` block before the `want=` dict attempts to resolve its UUID via `ls-groups`. Without this block, `ls-groups` will not list `Autosubmit Voters`, no UUID row is written to the `groups` file, and Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error — breaking the cutover entirely.
findings.location: What section ('Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict') and AC #3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the What section (after 'Register the group's UUID…'): 'In `setup-project.sh`, add an `Autosubmit Voters` provisioning block — identical in structure to the `feature-branch-drivers` block (lines 60–74) — that (a) creates the group if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --description "…" --group Administrators`), (b) idempotently adds `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot`, and (c) adds `"Autosubmit Voters"` to the `want=` dict so its UUID is resolved and written to the `groups` file before the `refs/meta/config` push.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether ADR-0042 needs to be created from scratch (no file exists in `docs/adr/`) and update AC6 to say 'author new ADR-0042' rather than amend an existing 'Security invariants section', or redirect the artifact to an existing doc.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 does not exist anywhere in the repository. AC6 references it as a pre-existing document to receive a new 'Security invariants section', but `docs/adr/` contains files only up to 0041-* entries and a search for '0042' returns no matches. This false-premise exclusion deletes the work of creating the ADR itself — if the file doesn't exist, the deliverable is authoring a full new ADR, not appending a section.
findings.location: AC6 / 'Security-invariants artifact' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records…' with 'Author `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` documenting…' to make clear this is a net-new file, not an amendment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the edit target (new script stanza, extended setup-project.sh block, or a new setup-autolander-bot.sh) that provisions the `autolander-bot` Gerrit account and adds it to `Autosubmit Voters`, analogous to how `setup-ci-service-account.sh` provisions the CI bot.
findings.finding: The plan names `autolander-bot` as the concrete service-account identity and states it must be placed in `Autosubmit Voters`, but no provisioning script or script stanza is identified as an edit target to CREATE the `autolander-bot` Gerrit account. The existing pattern (setup-ci-service-account.sh, service-user.sh) is not referenced or extended, leaving account creation an unenumerated required edit.
findings.location: What section: 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account `autolander-bot`' / AC1 and AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the named setup-project.sh edit to include not just the `want=` dict entry but also a `create-group` + `set-members` block for `Autosubmit Voters` (parallel to the feature-branch-drivers and Contributors blocks), so the group is guaranteed to exist before the refs/meta/config push.
findings.finding: The plan's `setup-project.sh` edit is described only as adding `Autosubmit Voters` to the `want=` UUID dict (line 123), but the script also needs a new `create-group` + `set-members` stanza (parallel to the `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` blocks at lines 60–107) to provision the group before the refs/meta/config push. Without the group-creation stanza, Gerrit will reject the push with 'unknown group', as the script's own comment at lines 51–52 explains.
findings.location: What section: 'Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict' / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a stanza to setup-project.sh section 1b: create `Autosubmit Voters` if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --owner Administrators`), then add `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot` (idempotent). Add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/adr/0042-<slug>.md` as an explicit named edit target (new file creation) in the plan's Scope or What section, clarifying that AC6 requires authoring this ADR file as part of the story's deliverables.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 is referenced in AC6 ('the ADR-0042 Security invariants section') as an edit/creation target, but this file does not exist anywhere in the repo (docs/adr/ contains ADRs 0001–0041; 0042 is absent). The plan never names ADR-0042 creation as an explicit edit target, even though AC6 requires authoring content within it.
findings.location: AC6: 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records the "non-gating label + bot-only vote ACL" invariant'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Author `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` recording the non-gating label + bot-only vote ACL invariant and its concrete verification checks (the grep and GET diff referenced in AC6).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` convergence block to `setup-project.sh` (analogous to the feature-branch-drivers and Contributors blocks) so the group is provisioned before the `want=` UUID resolution step, making the cutover idempotent from a clean state.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding 'Autosubmit Voters' to setup-project.sh's `want=` group-UUID dict, but does not specify the corresponding create-if-absent + membership-converge block for the group itself. Without a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` block (analogous to the existing feature-branch-drivers and Contributors blocks), the `want=` entry will fail to resolve a UUID — causing Gerrit to reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — on any run where the group hasn't been pre-created out-of-band.
findings.location: What section (setup-project.sh `want=` dict) / AC#3
findings.suggested_fix:
Insert a group-provisioning block in setup-project.sh (after the Contributors block, before step 2) following the existing pattern:
```bash
AV_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"
if $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AV_GROUP"; then
  echo "setup-project: group '$AV_GROUP' already exists" >&2
else
  echo "setup-project: creating group '$AV_GROUP'" >&2
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AV_GROUP" \
    --description "Accounts permitted to cast Autosubmit label votes (autolander-bot only)" \
    --owner Administrators
fi
$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --include Administrators >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
for _m in ${AUTOSUBMIT_VOTER_MEMBERS:-autolander-bot}; do
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --add "$_m" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
done
```
Then add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict on line 123.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix both REST verification endpoints in AC#5 and AC#6 to use the authenticated `/a/` prefix: `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and `GET /a/projects/rebar/access`, consistent with every other Gerrit REST call in the codebase.
findings.finding: Both REST verification endpoints cited in the ACs omit the required Gerrit authenticated prefix `/a/`: AC#5 writes `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and AC#6 writes `GET /projects/rebar/access`. Every existing Gerrit REST call in the codebase uses the `/a/` prefix (e.g., `GET /a/projects/rebar/config`, `GET /a/projects/rebar/branches/*`, `GET /a/accounts/self`). On Gerrit 3.14.1, the non-prefixed path returns 401 (or 404) for authenticated resources, so the operator running the post-cutover verification check will get a failure unrelated to the label — silently masking whether the label was actually applied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`) and AC#6 (`GET /projects/rebar/access` diff)
findings.suggested_fix: AC#5: `verified via GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` (with `-u admin:<token>` or bot credentials). AC#6: `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Scope section with AC #6: either add 'ADR-0042 security invariants update' to the Scope statement, or clarify whether ADR-0042 is a pre-existing document this story amends (and name the file path so the implementer knows where to write it).
findings.finding: The Scope section states 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' but AC #6 adds a distinct deliverable — authoring an ADR-0042 Security invariants section — that is not listed in Scope, Why, or What. This internal inconsistency could mislead an implementer into omitting the ADR artifact (because Scope says 'ONLY') or into treating it as in-scope without understanding its author/location.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC #6
findings.suggested_fix: Change Scope to: 'Config + ACL + cutover, plus the ADR-0042 Security invariants entry that records and verifies these controls. The service that reads the label is a separate story.' Also add a note in AC #6 naming the file path (e.g. `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label.md`) so the artifact location is unambiguous.

findings.finding: AC4 specifies `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` as the post-cutover verification oracle, but this specific REST endpoint surface has not been used anywhere in the codebase. The Gerrit REST labels API path (`/projects/{name}/labels/{labelName}`) is a distinct surface from the already-used `/projects/{name}/config` endpoint. The plan does not state whether the authenticated `/a/` prefix is required, what credential to use, or what response shape to expect — leaving the verification step Partially-verified and potentially silently broken if the endpoint returns 404, 403, or a different path on 3.14.1.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bare endpoint reference with a concrete curl pattern that mirrors the existing smoke-check.sh convention: `curl -sS -u "${BOT_USER}:${TOKEN}" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit"` and assert the response contains `"name": "Autosubmit"`. If that path 404s on 3.14.1, fall back to `GET /a/projects/rebar/config` (already verified in evidence docs) and inspect the `labels` key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the hedge 'if the low end is used' and state the default explicitly: include `value = -1 Do not land` unconditionally, since the declared range is `-1..+1`.
findings.finding: The `-1` value line is hedged with 'if the low end is used', leaving the executor to decide whether to declare `value = -1 Do not land` — but the label range is already fixed at `-1..+1`, so this is a defaultable decision the plan should resolve.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1: `value = -1 Do not land` (parenthetical hedge)
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC1 parenthetical to: `value = -1 Do not land` (required; range is -1..+1 so all three value lines must be declared).

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: explicitly declare whether `Administrators` can vote `label-Autosubmit` (add `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` if break-glass is required, or add a deliberate note that it is intentionally omitted) so the access posture at the label boundary is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 declares only a single ACL line (`label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`) without an explicit `Administrators` counterpart, leaving the Administrators vote posture at the label boundary undeclared. Every existing label in `project.config` (lines 46–51) explicitly pairs its group grant with `label-X = -1..+1 group Administrators`. An undeclared posture means implementors may either omit the Administrators grant (breaking break-glass scenarios where an admin must clear a stuck -1 vote) or silently inherit it via Gerrit's global Administrators capability — an untested assumption.
findings.location: AC2: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a second ACL line to AC2 mirroring the pattern from existing labels:
```
[access "refs/heads/*"]
  label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters
  label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators
```
And add a prose note in the What section explaining that Administrators retain break-glass vote capability, consistent with the LLM-Review and Verified label posture.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the conditional hedge on the `-1` value line: since the label range is fixed at `-1..+1`, declare `value = -1 Do not land` unconditionally in AC #1 and in `project.config`, matching the pattern of all existing labels (LLM-Review, Verified).
findings.finding: The Autosubmit label's `-1` value string is conditionally specified ('if the low end is used') despite the vote range being unconditionally fixed at `-1..+1`. This leaves the enum's low-end vocabulary undefined in `project.config` for any agent or downstream consumer that reads the label.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC #1: "value = -1 Do not land` if the low end is used"
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'value = -1 Do not land` if the low end is used' in AC #1 with the unconditional requirement: `value = -1 Do not land` must appear in `project.config` alongside `value = 0 No score` and `value = +1 Land this change`, since the label range `-1..+1` already commits to the low-end value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Note that the `git commit -q -m` string in `setup-project.sh` (line 148) must be updated to include `Autosubmit label + ACLs` when the other edits are applied.
findings.finding: The `setup-project.sh` hardcoded git commit message (line 148) lists the existing labels/features but will not reflect the new `Autosubmit` label addition, leaving the commit message stale. While minor, this is an unenumerated edit in a file the plan explicitly targets.
findings.location: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148 / What section naming setup-project.sh as an edit target
findings.suggested_fix: Append `+ Autosubmit label + ACLs` to the commit message string at setup-project.sh line 148.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief justification in the 'Why' section explaining why Gerrit's built-in Submit Queue / Change Queue (available in 3.14.x) is not used in place of the custom `Autosubmit` label, e.g., lack of per-group vote ACL scoping, incompatibility with the bot-lease model, or not being installed/enabled.
findings.finding: The plan correctly dismisses the Gerrit `autosubmit` submit-plugin but does not consider or dismiss Gerrit's built-in Submit Queue / Change Queue capability (available since Gerrit 3.7, present in 3.14.x), which is the closer architectural prior art for a machine-readable serialized-landing opt-in. Without acknowledging this alternative, the plan leaves open whether a custom label is the right primitive or whether the submit queue could serve the same role with less infrastructure.
findings.location: Why section / Name collision callout
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'Why' section: 'Gerrit 3.14's Submit Queue plugin was evaluated and rejected because [reason — e.g., it lacks per-group vote-ACL scoping for the bot identity / is not installed on this instance / does not model the lease-based intent the auto-lander requires]; the custom label is the minimal, ACL-controlled primitive that fits the lease model.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the live cutover end-to-end: the bot service account (`autolander-bot`) casts a `label Autosubmit=+1` vote on a scratch/test CL via `ssh gerrit review --label Autosubmit=+1 <change>,<ps>` (or equivalent REST `POST /changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/review`) authenticated as `autolander-bot`, and the vote is confirmed present via `GET /changes/{id}/detail` — proving group membership and ACL are wired correctly in the live instance.
findings.finding: The cutover to live `refs/meta/config` is verified only by reading REST endpoints (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff), which confirm the label and ACL exist in config — but no AC exercises the actual new code path in production: the bot service account (`autolander-bot`) casting a real `Autosubmit` label vote on a live CL. A mis-configured group membership (e.g., `autolander-bot` not actually in `Autosubmit Voters`) would pass all read-only checks yet fail silently when the bot tries to vote.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 and AC5 — live cutover verification
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5 or as new AC: 'Bot smoke-test: authenticated as `autolander-bot`, POST `{"labels":{"Autosubmit":1}}` to `POST /changes/<scratch-CL>/revisions/current/review`; assert HTTP 200 and `GET /changes/<scratch-CL>/detail` returns `"Autosubmit": {"value": 1}` for the bot account. Remove the scratch vote after verification.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Supply the explicit grep command for AC4's negative assertion in the ADR-0042 artifact, e.g.: `grep -E '^\s*submittedIf\s*=.*Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/project.config && echo FAIL || echo PASS` (exit 1 if any match found), anchored to avoid substring false-positives.
findings.finding: AC4's negative assertion — that the `Autosubmit` label is NOT referenced by any submit requirement — is stated as a criterion but has no explicit proving command anchored to it. The ADR-0042 section mentions a grep for `function = NoBlock` (positive) and 'no submit-requirement reference' (negative) but does not supply the actual grep pattern for the negative check, risking a false-negative where a stray submit-requirement line is missed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC4 and Security-invariants artifact in AC6
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC6 artifact description add: `! grep -E '^[[:space:]]*(submittedIf|submit-requirement)[^#]*Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/project.config` — command must exit 0 (no match) to pass the negative assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that ADR-0042 (created/updated in this story) serves as the full decision record for the Autosubmit label — covering rationale, non-gating choice, name-collision resolution, and bot-only ACL — or add an explicit ADR entry for these decisions alongside the security-invariants section.
findings.finding: The plan references ADR-0042 only for its 'Security invariants section' (AC6) but does not explicitly state that ADR-0042 also serves as—or that another ADR entry captures—the full architectural decision record for the custom Autosubmit label (rationale for non-gating, name-collision resolution, bot-only ACL design, relationship to ADR-0040). If ADR-0042 is solely a security-invariants artifact, the design decision itself is undocumented in the ADR ledger.
findings.location: AC6 / Security-invariants artifact section
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the AC6 artifact description to state: 'ADR-0042 is created as the decision record for the Autosubmit label, containing: (1) Design rationale section — why a project-authored label, why non-gating, name-collision warning vs Gerrit autosubmit plugin, relationship to ADR-0040 FFO; (2) Security invariants section — the grep/diff checks already specified.' This makes ADR-0042 the single durable home for both the decision and its invariants.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an idempotency contract to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict insertion: document (or enforce) that re-running the script with the same UUID is a no-op and that duplicate/conflicting entries are detected and rejected with a clear error.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict addition is idempotent under re-execution. If the script is run a second time (e.g., after a failed cutover attempt or during re-provisioning), adding a duplicate key to the dict may cause an error, a silent overwrite with a wrong UUID, or duplicate group registrations, depending on the shell implementation.
findings.location: ## What — '`setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict' / AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Use an associative-array upsert with an explicit duplicate-UUID check: if the group name already exists with a *different* UUID, abort with a descriptive error; if it exists with the same UUID, skip silently.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE section (even a stub) specifying: how to revoke the label from `project.config`, how to remove the `Autosubmit Voters` group from `setup-project.sh`, and what happens to existing label votes on open changes (e.g., they become inert / need a migration script).
findings.finding: The plan defines no RETIRE/cleanup path for the `Autosubmit` label or the `Autosubmit Voters` group. If the label is ever deprecated (e.g., the autolander is redesigned), existing `Autosubmit` votes on open changes will persist as orphan data, and the group will continue to be provisioned by `setup-project.sh` indefinitely.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## What

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the update-ownership and change-process for `Autosubmit Voters` membership (e.g., 'changes require a PR to `setup-project.sh` reviewed by the security team; membership is audited quarterly via `GET /groups/Autosubmit Voters/members`').
findings.finding: The plan does not define ongoing ownership of `Autosubmit Voters` group membership after initial provisioning. There is no specification of who is authorised to add or remove members (e.g., when the bot's service account rotates), under what process, and how to audit current membership.
findings.location: ## What — 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account `autolander-bot`, in group `Autosubmit Voters`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback procedure to the plan (AC5 or a new AC) that: (1) specifies the git-revert command against refs/meta/config, (2) asserts the post-rollback state (label absent from GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit, group still present but harmless), and (3) acknowledges that any Autosubmit votes cast before rollback remain in NoteDb and must be manually cleaned or are accepted as orphaned — attach a DATA LOSS RISK note if vote-record loss is possible.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a live cutover to `refs/meta/config` but never describes the post-rollback state or a rollback procedure. If the label is removed after any Autosubmit votes have been cast (e.g., a staged rollback), Gerrit retains orphaned vote records referencing the now-absent label, which can cause unpredictable API/UI behaviour and represents a DATA LOSS RISK for vote data.
findings.location: AC5: 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'Rollback procedure: `git push gerrit HEAD~1:refs/meta/config`; post-rollback state verified by confirming GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit returns 404. NOTE: Autosubmit votes cast before rollback remain as orphaned NoteDb entries — run `gerrit set-reviewers --remove autolander-bot` on affected changes or accept the orphaned records as inert.'

findings.finding: AC5 ('Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote gate') is process-framed ('security review') rather than a specific observable outcome, and has no concrete proving command; it cannot be evaluated deterministically in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC5: 'Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote (LLM-Review+Verified) gate'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5 with: 'Verify no submit requirement references `Autosubmit` (`grep -E "submittable|submitRequirement" infra/gerrit/project.config` returns no line containing `Autosubmit`), and `GET /projects/rebar/access` shows `LLM-Review` and `Verified` submit requirements unchanged from pre-cutover snapshot.'

findings.finding: AC2 claims the vote ACL is restricted to the bot, and that ordinary users and `addPatchSet` cannot forge it, but the named proving command (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`) returns label metadata only — it does NOT expose ACL grants, so the ACL restriction is never concretely verified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2 and AC4: 'verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC4 proving command for ACL verification with: `curl -s 'https://<gerrit>/a/projects/rebar/access' | jq -e '.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions["label-Autosubmit"].rules | keys == ["<bot-group-id>", "Administrators"]'` — this exercises the actual ACL, not just the label definition.

findings.finding: AC5 declares a security property ('the label cannot weaken the two-vote gate') but specifies no concrete verification method or evidence criteria, leaving the cutover gate-integrity check undeclared and potentially unverifiable at review time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote (`LLM-Review`+`Verified`) gate.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'Verified by: (a) `git diff` of `refs/meta/config` confirms zero changes to `[submit-requirements]`, `[label "LLM-Review"]`, and `[label "Verified"]` stanzas; (b) `GET /projects/rebar/submit_requirements` returns the same set as pre-cutover.'

findings.finding: The bot account / Gerrit group that receives the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL is referred to only as 'the auto-lander bot account' without naming the actual account or group. An executor writing the ACL stanza in project.config cannot proceed without this name.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2: 'label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Restricted to Gerrit service account `autosubmit-bot` (group `Autosubmit Voters`) and `Administrators`; stanza: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`.'

findings.finding: The `Autosubmit` label's value vocabulary (`value =` strings) is never defined in the plan. AC1 states '-1..+1' and 'function = NoBlock' but does not specify the named value lines (e.g., `value = -1 Do not land`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Land this change`). Every existing label in infra/gerrit/project.config (LLM-Review lines 105-107, Verified lines 139-141) carries explicit `value =` string definitions. The consuming bot story (explicitly deferred) will reference this label's schema; without value strings the definition is structurally incomplete as an agent-readable enum.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: '`Autosubmit` label added to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (`-1..+1`, `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition`…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to read: '`Autosubmit` label added to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (`-1..+1`, `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition`) with value strings: `value = -1 Do not land / value = 0 No autosubmit request / value = +1 Please land this change`.' This matches the vocabulary pattern of the existing labels and gives the consuming-bot story a canonical enum reference.

findings.finding: The plan never names the specific Gerrit group (or account) that will receive the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL — AC2 only says 'the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'. The existing setup-project.sh `want` dict (line 123) must include every group referenced in project.config so Gerrit can populate the `groups` file; an unnamed/uncreated group causes the refs/meta/config push to fail with 'unknown group'. Without knowing whether this is a new group (e.g. 'Auto-Landers'), an existing one ('Service Users'), or a direct account grant, the ACL cannot be implemented at minimum privilege.
findings.location: AC2: 'label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'
